title: Demonstrative Pronouns (This/That/These/Those)
description: Learn to use This, That, These, and Those — the four demonstrative pronouns/adjectives that help you point to people, objects, and ideas near or far. Essential for daily conversation, shopping, introductions, and professional communication.

subtopics.title: This — Singular Near
subtopics.description: This is my book. This pen is blue. Pointing to singular nearby things.

subtopics.title: That — Singular Far
subtopics.description: That is the Taj Mahal. That car is expensive. Pointing to distant things.

subtopics.title: These — Plural Near
subtopics.description: These are my friends. These books are mine. Multiple nearby items.

subtopics.title: Those — Plural Far
subtopics.description: Those are old buildings. Those people are teachers. Distant plural things.

subtopics.title: As Subject of Sentence
subtopics.description: This is important. Those were the days. Using demonstratives as subject.

subtopics.title: As Object / Adjective
subtopics.description: I want this shirt. Give me those keys. As object or describing a noun.

subtopics.title: In Questions
subtopics.description: Is this yours? Are those your friends? What is that? Asking questions.

subtopics.title: With Nouns (Demonstrative Adjectives)
subtopics.description: This book, that car, these students, those buildings — modifying nouns.

subtopics.title: This/That vs Articles (a/an/the)
subtopics.description: When to use this/that vs a/an/the — key differences explained.

subtopics.title: In Professional English
subtopics.description: Presentations, emails, meetings — This report, those figures, these results.

subtopics.title: Common Errors
subtopics.description: These informations (wrong!), This peoples (wrong!) — fixing typical mistakes.

learningObjectives: Understand the difference between near (this/these) and far (that/those)
Use this for singular near objects and people
Use that for singular far objects and ideas
Use these for plural near items
Use those for plural far items
Use demonstratives as subjects and objects
Use demonstratives as adjectives before nouns
Use demonstratives naturally in conversation and writing
Avoid common errors like these informations or this peoples
